title: Oracle user permissions misconfigured for Platform Engineering
description: Network members have no access to required tables in DynamoDB for the sandbox environment. Discovered during failed query.
resolution:
1. Review current grants for the affected database role.
2. Revoke excessive permissions immediately.
3. Grant only the minimum required permissions (principle of least privilege).
4. Create application-specific roles with scoped access.
5. Document the permission model and add to onboarding checklist.
6. Schedule quarterly access reviews.

title: CDN cache invalidation not propagating for Document Manager
description: After deploying updated static assets for Admin Console, CDN (Cloudflare) is still serving stale content. Cache invalidation was triggered but has not propagated globally. Users in US West still seeing old version.
resolution:
1. Verify invalidation request was submitted successfully.
2. Check invalidation status in CDN dashboard.
3. Force a wildcard invalidation if targeted invalidation isn't working.
4. Add cache-busting query parameters to asset URLs as a workaround.
5. Review cache TTL settings — reduce for frequently updated assets.
6. Implement versioned filenames for static assets.

title: VPN disconnections for remote employees
description: Multiple remote employees reporting Zscaler VPN disconnecting every 6 minutes. Affects 27 users across Europe region. Users lose access to internal resources. OS: macOS Ventura. Started 10 minutes ago.
resolution:
1. Check VPN gateway logs for disconnect reasons.
2. Verify VPN server capacity — may need scaling.
3. Check for MTU mismatch issues — set MTU to 1400 on client.
4. Update VPN client to the latest version.
5. Check if ISP-level throttling is affecting VPN traffic.
6. Enable VPN split-tunneling to reduce gateway load.

title: Docker container crash loop on staging-log-28
description: Docker container for Order Management is in a crash loop (CrashLoopBackOff) on dev-web-26. Logs show: ORA-01017. Environment: staging. Restarted 46 times in the last hour.
resolution:
1. Inspect container logs: `docker logs <container_id>`.
2. Check resource limits (memory/CPU) — container may be OOM killed.
3. Verify environment variables and config mounts.
4. Roll back to the previous known-good image.
5. Fix the root cause in application code and redeploy.

title: SSL/TLS misconfiguration on uat-cache-4
description: SSL Labs scan reports grade C for dev-cache-3. Issues: missing HSTS. This violates company security policy and may expose traffic to interception.
resolution:
1. Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 — enforce TLS 1.2+ only.
2. Remove weak cipher suites (RC4, DES, export ciphers).
3. Enable HSTS with a minimum 6-month max-age.
4. Fix certificate chain — include intermediate certificates.
5. Re-scan with SSL Labs to verify grade A/A+ achieved.

title: WiFi connectivity issues in Building A
description: Multiple employees reporting WiFi dropping or slow speeds in the new building. Speed test shows 5Mbps vs expected 382Mbps. 43 users affected. AP model: Ubiquiti.
resolution:
1. Check access point dashboard for the affected area.
2. Verify AP hardware status — reboot if unresponsive.
3. Check channel congestion and switch to less crowded channels.
4. Verify PoE switch is providing adequate power to the AP.
5. Deploy additional APs if coverage is insufficient.
6. Check for RF interference from nearby devices.

title: Order Management memory leak causing OOM crashes
description: Document Manager on uat-monitor-12 is consuming increasing memory over time, eventually hitting OOM and crashing. RSS grows from 464MB to 8GB within 9 hours. Environment: staging.
resolution:
1. Generate a heap dump for analysis: use appropriate profiler.
2. Identify leaking objects/connections using profiling tools.
3. Check for unclosed database connections, event listeners, or cache growth.
4. Apply fix and deploy with memory limits.
5. Set up memory usage alerting at 80% of container limit.

title: SSO login failing for Analytics Dashboard
description: Users cannot authenticate to SSO Portal via SSO (Ping Identity). Getting "SAML assertion invalid" error. 38 users affected. Started since yesterday ago.
resolution:
1. Check SSO provider dashboard for service status.
2. Verify SAML/OIDC configuration — check certificate expiry.
3. Test SSO login in incognito mode to rule out cache issues.
4. If certificate expired, upload the new IdP certificate.
5. Check clock sync between IdP and SP (SAML is time-sensitive).
6. Enable SSO debug logging and review assertion content.

title: Prometheus build failures on prod-app-4
description: PagerDuty pipeline has been failing for the last 2 hours on qa-log-17. Error: EACCES. All deployments to QA are blocked. Team Compliance is affected.
resolution:
1. Check ELK Stack system logs for root cause.
2. Restart the build agent/runner service.
3. Verify network connectivity to artifact repositories.
4. Clear workspace cache and retry the build.
5. If persistent, provision a new build agent.

title: Oracle replication lag at 683 seconds

5. Consider adding more replicas or upgrading instance size.

description: vikram.williams@company.com from QA requesting read-only access to GCP Lambda functions in production environment. Business justification: compliance audit.
resolution:

2. Create/identify an appropriate IAM role or group.
3. Grant scoped access following least-privilege principle.
4. Set an access expiration date if this is temporary access.
5. Document the access grant in the access management log.
6. Notify the user that access has been provisioned.

title: Kubernetes pod scheduling failures in QA
description: Multiple pods for Document Manager are stuck in Pending state in the production cluster. kubectl describe shows insufficient CPU/memory. The Database team cannot deploy.
resolution:
1. Check node resource utilization: `kubectl top nodes`.
2. Identify resource-hungry pods: `kubectl top pods --all-namespaces`.
3. Scale up worker node group or add new nodes.
4. Review and optimize resource requests/limits for pods.
5. Consider enabling cluster autoscaler.

title: API key generation request for Analytics Dashboard
description: alex.jones@company.com from ML needs API keys for Payment Gateway in DR environment. Purpose: third-party integration. Needs read-only access scope.
resolution:
1. Verify the request and business justification.
2. Generate API key with the minimum required scope.
3. Store the key in environment variables.
4. Set key expiration per security policy (max 90 days).
5. Document the API key and its purpose in the access log.
6. Share the key securely (never via email or chat).

title: MySQL deadlock detected in Ticket System
description: Frequent deadlocks occurring in Cassandra when Monitoring Dashboard processes user session writes. 84 deadlocks in the last hour. Transaction rollbacks affecting user experience.
resolution:
1. Analyze deadlock logs to identify the conflicting transactions.
2. Review application locking order — ensure consistent acquisition order.
3. Reduce transaction scope and duration.
4. Add appropriate row-level locking hints.
5. Implement retry logic for deadlock errors in application code.
6. Consider optimistic locking where possible.

title: Database migration failed for Analytics Dashboard
description: Schema migration for API Gateway on MongoDB (staging) failed mid-way. Migration altered an index. The database is now in an inconsistent state. Rollback was not attempted.
resolution:
1. Assess current schema state vs expected state.
2. If possible, manually complete or roll back the failed migration.
3. Fix the migration script for idempotency.
4. Restore from the pre-migration backup if state is unrecoverable.
5. Re-run the migration in a staging environment first.
6. Implement migration dry-run and backup as pre-deployment steps.

title: Unauthorized access to Azure console detected
description: CloudTrail/Activity Log shows unauthorized API calls from an unrecognized IP 14.89.216.55 using IAM user devops-bot. Actions include: CreateUser. Credentials may be compromised.
resolution:
1. Immediately rotate/deactivate the compromised IAM credentials.
2. Review CloudTrail logs for all actions taken by the compromised user.
3. Revert any unauthorized changes (new users, policies, resources).
4. Enable MFA on all IAM accounts if not already enabled.
5. Implement IP-based access restrictions for console access.
6. Conduct a full IAM access review.

title: DNS resolution failures for internal services
description: Internal DNS resolution failing intermittently. *.svc.cluster.local not resolving. Affecting 13 services including Analytics Dashboard. DNS server: 23.189.252.49. Started since this morning ago.
resolution:
1. Check DNS server health and query logs.
2. Verify DNS zone configurations and SOA records.
3. Flush DNS cache on affected clients: `ipconfig /flushdns` or equivalent.
4. Check if DNS server is overwhelmed — add secondary DNS.
5. Verify network ACLs are not blocking DNS (UDP/TCP 53).
6. Test resolution with `nslookup` and `dig` for debugging.

title: Phishing email reported by SRE team members
description: Multiple users from Frontend reported a phishing email impersonating Microsoft. Email contains a malicious link. 12 users may have interacted with it.
resolution:
1. Block the sender domain at the email gateway.
2. Search mailboxes for the phishing email and delete all instances.
3. Identify users who clicked the link or opened the attachment.
4. Force password resets for affected users.
5. Run endpoint scans on machines that interacted with the email.
6. Send a company-wide phishing awareness reminder.

title: Redis storage reaching capacity on qa-worker-29
description: Oracle data directory on dev-proxy-12 is at 89% capacity (114GB / 279GB). If it fills up, the database will go read-only or crash. Invoice System and Backend will be affected. Growth rate: 4GB/day.
resolution:
1. Identify the largest tables and indexes consuming space.
2. Run VACUUM/OPTIMIZE to reclaim dead tuple space.
3. Archive or delete data older than the retention policy.
4. Expand disk/EBS volume size.
5. Implement table partitioning for time-series data.
6. Set up storage alerts at 80% and 90% thresholds.

title: MariaDB memory at 96% utilization

resolution:

2. Kill any runaway queries that are not critical.
3. Vertically scale the instance (upgrade CPU/memory).
4. Optimize the top 5 most expensive queries.
5. Implement query timeout settings.
6. Set up proactive alerting at 80% utilization threshold.

title: dev-monitor-18 disk space critical
description: Server prod-worker-26 in QA environment is at 95% disk usage. Datadog builds are failing due to insufficient space. Affecting the Identity team. OS: RHEL 9.

title: BGP peering session down with ISP

title: Data corruption detected in Cassandra for CRM Portal
description: Order Management reporting inconsistent data from MongoDB. checksum mismatches found. Affects 9575 records. Likely caused by application bug.
resolution:
1. Immediately take a backup of the current (corrupted) state for analysis.
2. Identify the scope of corrupted records.
3. If recent, restore from the last known good backup.
4. Apply transaction logs to recover data up to the corruption point.
5. Fix the root cause (migration script, application bug, etc.).
6. Implement data integrity checks as a scheduled job.

title: Cassandra connection pool exhausted on prod-web-3
description: MariaDB on staging-proxy-6 has exhausted its connection pool (129/104 connections active). Document Manager is returning connection timeout errors. Environment: sandbox. Started 10 minutes ago.
resolution:
1. Identify connections holding open transactions: check `pg_stat_activity` or equivalent.
2. Terminate idle-in-transaction connections older than 5 minutes.
3. Increase the connection pool size if usage is legitimately high.
4. Review application code for connection leaks (unclosed connections).
5. Implement connection pooling middleware (PgBouncer/ProxySQL).

title: Privilege escalation detected in QA environment
description: SIEM alert: user john.sharma@company.com escalated privileges from viewer to admin role without authorization in pre-prod. Role binding change detected.
resolution:

3. Check for any actions performed with elevated access.

6. Review IAM policies to prevent similar escalation paths.

title: BGP peering session down with ISP
description: BGP peering session with secondary ISP is down for 10 minutes. Prefix advertisements withdrawn. Traffic failover to secondary ISP is active. External-facing services including Inventory Manager may be affected.
resolution:
1. Check router BGP neighbor status and logs.
2. Verify physical layer — check for fiber/port issues.
3. Contact the ISP NOC with BGP session details.
4. If hardware issue, move to backup port/router.
5. Verify failover routing is working correctly.
6. After restoration, verify all prefixes are being advertised.

title: Docker container crash loop on qa-web-28

5. Fix the root cause in application code and redeploy.

title: NTP clock drift detected on uat-api-26

resolution:
1. Check current time offset: `timedatectl status`.
2. Force NTP sync: `ntpdate -u pool.ntp.org` or `chronyc makestep`.
3. Verify NTP service is running and configured correctly.
4. Add NTP monitoring to the alerting infrastructure.
5. Check if firewall is blocking NTP (UDP 123).

title: DynamoDB user permissions misconfigured for DevOps

3. Grant only the minimum required permissions (principle of least privilege).
4. Create application-specific roles with scoped access.
5. Document the permission model and add to onboarding checklist.
6. Schedule quarterly access reviews.

title: DNS resolution failures for internal services

resolution:
1. Check DNS server health and query logs.
2. Verify DNS zone configurations and SOA records.
3. Flush DNS cache on affected clients: `ipconfig /flushdns` or equivalent.
4. Check if DNS server is overwhelmed — add secondary DNS.
5. Verify network ACLs are not blocking DNS (UDP/TCP 53).
6. Test resolution with `nslookup` and `dig` for debugging.

title: CDN cache invalidation not propagating for Ticket System

resolution:
1. Verify invalidation request was submitted successfully.
2. Check invalidation status in CDN dashboard.
3. Force a wildcard invalidation if targeted invalidation isn't working.
4. Add cache-busting query parameters to asset URLs as a workaround.
5. Review cache TTL settings — reduce for frequently updated assets.
6. Implement versioned filenames for static assets.

description: Traefik load balancer reporting 4 out of 13 backend targets as unhealthy for Monitoring Dashboard. Health check path: /healthz. Traffic being sent to fewer instances, causing overload.
resolution:
1. Check health check endpoint on failing instances — curl manually.
2. Verify security groups allow health check traffic from the load balancer.
3. Check if application is listening on the expected port.
4. Review application health check logic — it may be failing on DB/dependency checks.
5. Restart unhealthy instances and monitor.
6. Increase health check timeout/threshold to avoid flapping.

title: SSO login failing for Invoice System
description: Users cannot authenticate to Ticket System via SSO (Ping Identity). Getting "Redirect loop" error. 80 users affected. Started 10 minutes ago.
resolution:
1. Check SSO provider dashboard for service status.
2. Verify SAML/OIDC configuration — check certificate expiry.
3. Test SSO login in incognito mode to rule out cache issues.
4. If certificate expired, upload the new IdP certificate.
5. Check clock sync between IdP and SP (SAML is time-sensitive).
6. Enable SSO debug logging and review assertion content.

title: Firewall rule blocking traffic to CI/CD Pipeline
description: New firewall rule is inadvertently blocking outbound traffic on port 8443 to Reporting Engine on qa-app-19. Deployed 2 hours ago as part of compliance update. Network team affected.
resolution:
1. Identify the blocking firewall rule from logs.
2. Add an exception rule for the required traffic.
3. Verify the fix with connection tests (telnet/nc).
4. Review the firewall change process to include impact assessment.
5. Document the exception in the network security policy.
6. Implement a change review process for firewall modifications.

title: Network latency spike between eastus and eu-west-1
description: Cross-region network latency between AWS eastus and ap-south-1 spiked from 24ms to 225ms. Affecting Notification Hub which has components in both regions. Mobile reporting degraded performance.
resolution:
1. Check GCP health dashboard for network issues.
2. Run traceroute/mtr to identify the network hop causing latency.
3. If provider issue, open a support case with GCP.
4. Enable caching at the edge to reduce cross-region calls.
5. Consider deploying service replicas in the same region.
6. Implement circuit breakers for cross-region dependencies.

title: Malware detected on endpoint qa-monitor-30
description: Endpoint protection (Carbon Black) flagged a ransomware on qa-proxy-10 used by raj.taylor@company.com. File: update.exe. Status: partially removed.
resolution:
1. Immediately isolate the affected machine from the network.
2. Run a full system scan with the endpoint protection tool.
3. Check if the malware has spread to other machines on the network.
4. Reset all credentials used on the affected machine.
5. Reimage the machine if the infection is severe.
6. Submit the malware sample to the threat intelligence team.

title: Terraform state lock stuck for sandbox infrastructure
description: Terraform state is locked for the UAT infrastructure in AWS us-east-1. Lock ID exists in DynamoDB/Cloud Storage. No one can apply infrastructure changes. Blocking Mobile.
resolution:
1. Identify who holds the lock: check the lock metadata.
2. If the previous apply crashed, force-unlock: `terraform force-unlock <LOCK_ID>`.
3. Verify state file integrity after unlocking.
4. Run `terraform plan` to ensure no drift.
5. Implement CI-based Terraform runs to prevent manual lock conflicts.

title: CI/CD Pipeline memory leak causing OOM crashes
description: Order Management on prod-worker-6 is consuming increasing memory over time, eventually hitting OOM and crashing. RSS grows from 435MB to 8GB within 9 hours. Environment: staging.
resolution:
1. Generate a heap dump for analysis: use appropriate profiler.
2. Identify leaking objects/connections using profiling tools.
3. Check for unclosed database connections, event listeners, or cache growth.
4. Apply fix and deploy with memory limits.
5. Set up memory usage alerting at 80% of container limit.

title: Mobile app crashing on iOS for Analytics Dashboard
description: The mobile app for Email Service is crashing immediately on launch for iOS 17 users. Crash reports show EACCES. Approximately 1680 crash reports in the last 2 hours.
resolution:

4. If API-related, add backward compatibility on the server side.
5. Push the fixed build to App Store/Play Store as an expedited review.

title: Customer Portal search functionality returning no results
description: Search in Document Manager returns empty results for all queries. Algolia index appears to be empty. Reported by 7 users from Database.
resolution:
1. Check search engine cluster health and index status.
2. Verify the data sync pipeline between the primary database and search index.
3. Rebuild the search index from the source database.
4. Verify search query parsing and filtering logic.
5. Monitor indexing lag to prevent future occurrences.

title: BGP peering session down with ISP
description: BGP peering session with backup ISP is down for since this morning. Prefix advertisements withdrawn. Traffic failover to backup link is partial. External-facing services including Deployment Manager may be affected.
resolution:
1. Check router BGP neighbor status and logs.
2. Verify physical layer — check for fiber/port issues.
3. Contact the ISP NOC with BGP session details.
4. If hardware issue, move to backup port/router.
5. Verify failover routing is working correctly.
6. After restoration, verify all prefixes are being advertised.

title: DNS resolution failures for internal services

5. Verify network ACLs are not blocking DNS (UDP/TCP 53).

title: uat-cache-5 disk space critical

resolution:
1. SSH into the server and identify large files with `du -sh /var/*`.
2. Prune old Docker images: `docker system prune -af`.
3. Clear old build artifacts and logs older than 30 days.
4. Expand EBS/disk volume by 51GB.
5. Set up disk space monitoring alert at 80% threshold.

title: NTP clock drift detected on uat-auth-23
description: Clock drift of 135 seconds detected on prod-monitor-20 running CentOS Stream 9. This is causing authentication failures and log timestamp mismatches for Inventory Manager.
resolution:
1. Check current time offset: `timedatectl status`.
2. Force NTP sync: `ntpdate -u pool.ntp.org` or `chronyc makestep`.
3. Verify NTP service is running and configured correctly.
4. Add NTP monitoring to the alerting infrastructure.
5. Check if firewall is blocking NTP (UDP 123).

title: SQL Server storage reaching capacity on prod-auth-27
description: DynamoDB data directory on prod-monitor-4 is at 88% capacity (294GB / 140GB). If it fills up, the database will go read-only or crash. CI/CD Pipeline and DevOps will be affected. Growth rate: 6GB/day.
resolution:
1. Identify the largest tables and indexes consuming space.
2. Run VACUUM/OPTIMIZE to reclaim dead tuple space.
3. Archive or delete data older than the retention policy.
4. Expand disk/EBS volume size.
5. Implement table partitioning for time-series data.
6. Set up storage alerts at 80% and 90% thresholds.

title: Scheduled job failures in Document Manager

5. Fix the root cause, clear any stuck/orphaned job locks, and re-enable scheduling.

title: Cassandra deadlock detected in HR Dashboard

resolution:

2. Review application locking order — ensure consistent acquisition order.
3. Reduce transaction scope and duration.
4. Add appropriate row-level locking hints.
5. Implement retry logic for deadlock errors in application code.
6. Consider optimistic locking where possible.

description: Cross-region network latency between AWS eastus and eastus spiked from 16ms to 419ms. Affecting Payment Gateway which has components in both regions. Frontend reporting degraded performance.
resolution:
1. Check AWS health dashboard for network issues.
2. Run traceroute/mtr to identify the network hop causing latency.
3. If provider issue, open a support case with Azure.
4. Enable caching at the edge to reduce cross-region calls.
5. Consider deploying service replicas in the same region.
6. Implement circuit breakers for cross-region dependencies.

title: Slow query degrading Elasticsearch performance
description: A slow query on MySQL (staging-log-8) is running for 562 seconds, causing table locks and degrading CI/CD Pipeline performance. Query involves missing index on JOIN column. Affecting 93 concurrent users.
resolution:
1. Identify the slow query from the slow query log.
2. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE to diagnose the execution plan.
3. Add appropriate indexes based on the query pattern.
4. Optimize the query (add WHERE filters, limit results, fix JOINs).
5. Consider query caching for frequently executed queries.
6. Set up slow query alerting with 21s threshold.

title: MFA enrollment issues for SRE users
description: 15 users from ML unable to complete MFA enrollment with YubiKey. QR code not scanning. Users cannot access MFA-protected resources.
resolution:

3. Walk users through enrollment step-by-step.
4. Verify device time is accurate (TOTP is time-sensitive).
5. Offer alternative MFA methods (SMS/email) as backup.
6. If hardware token issue, replace the security key.

title: Payment Gateway file upload failing with size limit error

resolution:

2. Update application-level body parser limits.
3. Verify cloud storage (S3/GCS) bucket permissions.

5. Add user-friendly error messages for size limit violations.

title: SQL Server replication lag at 704 seconds

title: DynamoDB backup job failed for QA

title: NTP clock drift detected on dev-log-13

resolution:

3. Verify NTP service is running and configured correctly.
4. Add NTP monitoring to the alerting infrastructure.

description: Server prod-app-30 in UAT environment is at 90% disk usage. Jenkins builds are failing due to insufficient space. Affecting the QA team. OS: RHEL 9.

title: SSL/TLS misconfiguration on uat-monitor-8
description: SSL Labs scan reports grade F for uat-web-21. Issues: TLS 1.0/1.1 enabled. This violates company security policy and may expose traffic to interception.
resolution:
1. Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 — enforce TLS 1.2+ only.
2. Remove weak cipher suites (RC4, DES, export ciphers).
3. Enable HSTS with a minimum 6-month max-age.
4. Fix certificate chain — include intermediate certificates.
5. Re-scan with SSL Labs to verify grade A/A+ achieved.

5. Implement connection pooling middleware (PgBouncer/ProxySQL).

title: Slow query degrading Cassandra performance

3. Add appropriate indexes based on the query pattern.

5. Consider query caching for frequently executed queries.
6. Set up slow query alerting with 29s threshold.

title: NTP clock drift detected on prod-worker-20

resolution:

4. Add NTP monitoring to the alerting infrastructure.
5. Check if firewall is blocking NTP (UDP 123).

1. Review and categorize current alert rules.
2. Increase alert thresholds for non-critical metrics.
3. Add alert grouping and deduplication rules.
4. Implement alert silencing for known maintenance windows.
5. Create tiered alerting: P1 pages, P2 Slack, P3 dashboard only.

title: CDN cache invalidation not propagating for SSO Portal
description: After deploying updated static assets for CRM Portal, CDN (Fastly) is still serving stale content. Cache invalidation was triggered but has not propagated globally. Users in APAC still seeing old version.
resolution:
1. Verify invalidation request was submitted successfully.
2. Check invalidation status in CDN dashboard.
3. Force a wildcard invalidation if targeted invalidation isn't working.
4. Add cache-busting query parameters to asset URLs as a workaround.
5. Review cache TTL settings — reduce for frequently updated assets.
6. Implement versioned filenames for static assets.

title: Database migration failed for Notification Hub
description: Schema migration for Chat Service on MongoDB (DR) failed mid-way. Migration altered an index. The database is now in an inconsistent state. Rollback was not attempted.
resolution:
1. Assess current schema state vs expected state.
2. If possible, manually complete or roll back the failed migration.
3. Fix the migration script for idempotency.
4. Restore from the pre-migration backup if state is unrecoverable.
5. Re-run the migration in a staging environment first.
6. Implement migration dry-run and backup as pre-deployment steps.

title: Slow query degrading SQL Server performance

resolution:

3. Add appropriate indexes based on the query pattern.
4. Optimize the query (add WHERE filters, limit results, fix JOINs).
5. Consider query caching for frequently executed queries.
6. Set up slow query alerting with 7s threshold.

title: Data corruption detected in MariaDB for CI/CD Pipeline

3. Enable HSTS with a minimum 6-month max-age.
4. Fix certificate chain — include intermediate certificates.
5. Re-scan with SSL Labs to verify grade A/A+ achieved.

title: Elasticsearch storage reaching capacity on prod-worker-9

resolution:

2. Run VACUUM/OPTIMIZE to reclaim dead tuple space.
3. Archive or delete data older than the retention policy.
4. Expand disk/EBS volume size.
5. Implement table partitioning for time-series data.
6. Set up storage alerts at 80% and 90% thresholds.

2. Run a full system scan with the endpoint protection tool.
3. Check if the malware has spread to other machines on the network.
4. Reset all credentials used on the affected machine.
5. Reimage the machine if the infection is severe.
6. Submit the malware sample to the threat intelligence team.

title: BGP peering session down with ISP
description: BGP peering session with secondary ISP is down for since this morning. Prefix advertisements withdrawn. Traffic failover to backup link is not working. External-facing services including SSO Portal may be affected.
resolution:
1. Check router BGP neighbor status and logs.
2. Verify physical layer — check for fiber/port issues.
3. Contact the ISP NOC with BGP session details.
4. If hardware issue, move to backup port/router.
5. Verify failover routing is working correctly.
6. After restoration, verify all prefixes are being advertised.

title: SSL/TLS misconfiguration on qa-proxy-22
description: SSL Labs scan reports grade F for uat-monitor-5. Issues: TLS 1.0/1.1 enabled. This violates company security policy and may expose traffic to interception.
resolution:
1. Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1 — enforce TLS 1.2+ only.
2. Remove weak cipher suites (RC4, DES, export ciphers).
3. Enable HSTS with a minimum 6-month max-age.
4. Fix certificate chain — include intermediate certificates.
5. Re-scan with SSL Labs to verify grade A/A+ achieved.

title: Azure service quota exceeded in westeurope
description: Hitting service quota limits for EBS volumes in GCP us-west-2. Cannot provision new resources for Backend.
resolution:
1. Review current quota usage in Azure console.
2. Submit a service quota increase request.
3. Clean up unused/orphaned resources.
4. Consider using a different region for non-critical workloads.
5. Implement resource tagging and lifecycle policies.

title: Suspicious login attempts detected for sarah.anderson@company.com
description: Security alert: 191 failed login attempts detected for account neha.gupta@company.com from IP 179.52.66.254 (Brazil). Account may be under brute-force attack. Current status: still active.
resolution:
1. Temporarily lock the affected user account.
2. Block the source IP at the WAF/firewall level.
3. Reset the user's password and require MFA reenrollment.
4. Review login audit logs for any successful unauthorized access.
5. Notify the user and their manager about the incident.
6. Add the IP range to the threat intelligence blocklist.

title: Network latency spike between eu-central-1 and westeurope
description: Cross-region network latency between GCP us-central1 and us-east-1 spiked from 19ms to 710ms. Affecting API Gateway which has components in both regions. Network reporting degraded performance.
resolution:
1. Check AWS health dashboard for network issues.
2. Run traceroute/mtr to identify the network hop causing latency.
3. If provider issue, open a support case with GCP.
4. Enable caching at the edge to reduce cross-region calls.
5. Consider deploying service replicas in the same region.
6. Implement circuit breakers for cross-region dependencies.

title: SSO login failing for Notification Hub
description: Users cannot authenticate to Notification Hub via SSO (OneLogin). Getting "SAML assertion invalid" error. 70 users affected. Started 1 hour ago.
resolution:
1. Check SSO provider dashboard for service status.
2. Verify SAML/OIDC configuration — check certificate expiry.
3. Test SSO login in incognito mode to rule out cache issues.
4. If certificate expired, upload the new IdP certificate.
5. Check clock sync between IdP and SP (SAML is time-sensitive).
6. Enable SSO debug logging and review assertion content.

title: New employee access provisioning for Frontend
description: New employee joining Compliance needs access to: Office 365, Confluence, Monitoring Dashboard, CRM, AWS Console. Start date: tomorrow. Manager has approved.
resolution:
1. Create Active Directory account with standard group memberships.
2. Assign Office 365 license and create mailbox.
3. Add to relevant Slack channels and GitHub teams.
4. Provision access to requested applications per team role template.
5. Set up VPN profile and send credentials.
6. Send welcome email with access details and onboarding guide.

title: DynamoDB user permissions misconfigured for Platform Engineering
description: Identity members have excessive read/write access in MongoDB for the UAT environment. Discovered during failed query.
resolution:
1. Review current grants for the affected database role.
2. Revoke excessive permissions immediately.
3. Grant only the minimum required permissions (principle of least privilege).
4. Create application-specific roles with scoped access.
5. Document the permission model and add to onboarding checklist.
6. Schedule quarterly access reviews.

title: DynamoDB connection pool exhausted on dev-proxy-24
description: MongoDB on qa-app-25 has exhausted its connection pool (121/179 connections active). Email Service is returning connection timeout errors. Environment: sandbox. Started since yesterday ago.
resolution:
1. Identify connections holding open transactions: check `pg_stat_activity` or equivalent.
2. Terminate idle-in-transaction connections older than 5 minutes.
3. Increase the connection pool size if usage is legitimately high.
4. Review application code for connection leaks (unclosed connections).
5. Implement connection pooling middleware (PgBouncer/ProxySQL).

title: Service account key rotation overdue for AWS
description: 9 service account keys in GCP have not been rotated in 364 days, violating the 60-day rotation policy. Accounts used by: Invoice System, PagerDuty. Compliance audit flagged this as a finding.
resolution:
1. Generate new keys for each service account.
2. Update the keys in all dependent services, CI/CD pipelines, and secret managers.
3. Revoke the old keys after confirming new keys work.
4. Implement automated key rotation via Vault.
5. Set up alerts for key age exceeding policy threshold.

title: Redis CPU at 100% utilization
description: SQL Server instance dev-monitor-27 has been at 97% IOPS utilization for 5 minutes. Query queue is building up. Invoice System response times severely degraded. Environment: DR.
resolution:
1. Identify the resource-consuming queries/processes.
2. Kill any runaway queries that are not critical.
3. Vertically scale the instance (upgrade CPU/memory).
4. Optimize the top 5 most expensive queries.
5. Implement query timeout settings.
6. Set up proactive alerting at 80% utilization threshold.

title: Phishing email reported by SRE team members

resolution:

3. Identify users who clicked the link or opened the attachment.
4. Force password resets for affected users.
5. Run endpoint scans on machines that interacted with the email.
6. Send a company-wide phishing awareness reminder.

title: Data exfiltration alert from DLP system

4. Engage legal/compliance team for incident documentation.
5. Implement additional DLP rules to prevent recurrence.
6. File a data breach notification if required by regulations.

title: prod-worker-8 disk space critical

resolution:

2. Prune old Docker images: `docker system prune -af`.
3. Clear old build artifacts and logs older than 30 days.
4. Expand EBS/disk volume by 95GB.
5. Set up disk space monitoring alert at 80% threshold.

title: Slow query degrading MariaDB performance

resolution:

2. Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE to diagnose the execution plan.
3. Add appropriate indexes based on the query pattern.
4. Optimize the query (add WHERE filters, limit results, fix JOINs).
5. Consider query caching for frequently executed queries.
6. Set up slow query alerting with 24s threshold.

5. Fix the root cause (migration script, application bug, etc.).

title: BGP peering session down with ISP

title: BGP peering session down with ISP

title: Terraform state lock stuck for production infrastructure

3. Verify state file integrity after unlocking.
4. Run `terraform plan` to ensure no drift.
5. Implement CI-based Terraform runs to prevent manual lock conflicts.

5. Implement table partitioning for time-series data.
6. Set up storage alerts at 80% and 90% thresholds.

3. Flush DNS cache on affected clients: `ipconfig /flushdns` or equivalent.
4. Check if DNS server is overwhelmed — add secondary DNS.
5. Verify network ACLs are not blocking DNS (UDP/TCP 53).
6. Test resolution with `nslookup` and `dig` for debugging.

title: Network latency spike between us-central1 and eu-central-1
description: Cross-region network latency between AWS ap-south-1 and us-west-2 spiked from 29ms to 261ms. Affecting Admin Console which has components in both regions. Frontend reporting degraded performance.
resolution:
1. Check Azure health dashboard for network issues.
2. Run traceroute/mtr to identify the network hop causing latency.
3. If provider issue, open a support case with Azure.
4. Enable caching at the edge to reduce cross-region calls.
5. Consider deploying service replicas in the same region.
6. Implement circuit breakers for cross-region dependencies.

title: Ticket System file upload failing with size limit error
description: Users cannot upload files larger than 10MB in CI/CD Pipeline. Error: "Request entity too large" or NullPointerException. This is blocking Compliance from uploading contracts.
resolution:
1. Check and increase upload size limits in the web server config (nginx/Apache).
2. Update application-level body parser limits.
3. Verify cloud storage (S3/GCS) bucket permissions.
4. Consider implementing multipart/chunked upload for large files.
5. Add user-friendly error messages for size limit violations.

title: Azure service quota exceeded in westeurope
description: Hitting service quota limits for NAT Gateways in Azure eu-west-1. Cannot provision new resources for Cloud.
resolution:
1. Review current quota usage in Azure console.
2. Submit a service quota increase request.
3. Clean up unused/orphaned resources.
4. Consider using a different region for non-critical workloads.
5. Implement resource tagging and lifecycle policies.

title: PostgreSQL replication lag at 648 seconds
description: Read replica of PostgreSQL on prod-api-22 has a replication lag of 2125 seconds. Admin Console reads from the replica are returning stale data. This is affecting user profile reads. Environment: DR.
resolution:
1. Check replica status and identify the bottleneck.
2. Review write throughput on the primary — check for bulk operations.
3. Ensure the replica has sufficient IOPS and network bandwidth.
4. Temporarily direct reads to the primary if lag is critical.
5. Consider adding more replicas or upgrading instance size.
6. Set up replication lag monitoring with 60s threshold alerts.

title: Reporting Engine file upload failing with size limit error
description: Users cannot upload files larger than 5MB in Chat Service. Error: "Request entity too large" or EPERM. This is blocking Security from uploading spreadsheets.
resolution:
1. Check and increase upload size limits in the web server config (nginx/Apache).
2. Update application-level body parser limits.
3. Verify cloud storage (S3/GCS) bucket permissions.
4. Consider implementing multipart/chunked upload for large files.
5. Add user-friendly error messages for size limit violations.

title: Phishing email reported by Cloud team members
description: Multiple users from Compliance reported a phishing email impersonating Microsoft. Email contains a fake invoice. 27 users may have interacted with it.
resolution:
1. Block the sender domain at the email gateway.
2. Search mailboxes for the phishing email and delete all instances.
3. Identify users who clicked the link or opened the attachment.
4. Force password resets for affected users.
5. Run endpoint scans on machines that interacted with the email.
6. Send a company-wide phishing awareness reminder.

title: Redis backup job failed for sandbox
description: Nightly Redis backup for UAT environment failed at upload to S3. Error: permission denied on backup directory. Last successful backup was 3 days ago.
resolution:
1. Check backup job logs for the specific error.
2. Verify backup storage has sufficient space.
3. Ensure backup service account has correct permissions.
4. Run a manual backup immediately to restore the backup chain.
5. Verify backup integrity with a test restore.
6. Set up backup failure alerts to catch issues within 24 hours.

title: Load balancer health check failures for Inventory Manager
description: ALB load balancer reporting 3 out of 9 backend targets as unhealthy for Admin Console. Health check path: /api/status. Traffic being sent to fewer instances, causing overload.
resolution:
1. Check health check endpoint on failing instances — curl manually.
2. Verify security groups allow health check traffic from the load balancer.
3. Check if application is listening on the expected port.
4. Review application health check logic — it may be failing on DB/dependency checks.
5. Restart unhealthy instances and monitor.
6. Increase health check timeout/threshold to avoid flapping.

title: API response times degraded for Customer Portal
description: Average API response time for HR Dashboard has increased from 105ms to 6s. Users reporting slow page loads. Security dashboard shows timeout spikes. Started since this morning ago.
resolution:
1. Check APM/tracing for slowest endpoints.
2. Identify bottleneck: database queries, external API calls, or compute.
3. Add database query indexes if N+1 queries detected.
4. Implement caching for frequently accessed data.
5. Scale horizontally if load has genuinely increased.

title: Customer Portal memory leak causing OOM crashes
description: CRM Portal on staging-proxy-10 is consuming increasing memory over time, eventually hitting OOM and crashing. RSS grows from 492MB to 4GB within 11 hours. Environment: pre-prod.
resolution:
1. Generate a heap dump for analysis: use appropriate profiler.
2. Identify leaking objects/connections using profiling tools.
3. Check for unclosed database connections, event listeners, or cache growth.
4. Apply fix and deploy with memory limits.
5. Set up memory usage alerting at 80% of container limit.

title: Group membership update for Security
description: 5 users need to be removed from the read-only group for HR Dashboard. Requested by team lead. Reason: project assignment.
resolution:
1. Verify the request with the team lead/manager.
2. Review the group permissions to ensure appropriateness.
3. Add/remove the specified users from the group.
4. Verify each user's access is updated correctly.
5. Notify affected users of the change.
6. Update the team access matrix documentation.

title: Grafana build failures on staging-worker-7
description: GitHub Actions pipeline has been failing for the last since this morning on prod-cache-9. Error: SEGFAULT. All deployments to pre-prod are blocked. Team Platform Engineering is affected.
resolution:
1. Check Consul system logs for root cause.
2. Restart the build agent/runner service.
3. Verify network connectivity to artifact repositories.
4. Clear workspace cache and retry the build.
5. If persistent, provision a new build agent.

title: Payment Gateway login page broken after update
description: Users cannot log into HR Dashboard after the latest release. Login form submits but returns a blank page or ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED. Affecting all users on Firefox 121, macOS Sonoma. SSO redirect flow is broken.
resolution:
1. Check frontend console errors in the browser.
2. Verify SSO/OAuth callback URLs are correctly configured.
3. Check if the auth service/identity provider is reachable.
4. Review recent code changes to the login flow.
5. Roll back the frontend build if needed and redeploy.

title: MFA enrollment issues for Frontend users
description: 18 users from Database unable to complete MFA enrollment with Google Authenticator. QR code not scanning. Users cannot access MFA-protected resources.
resolution:
1. Reset MFA enrollment for the affected users.
2. Generate new QR codes/activation links.
3. Walk users through enrollment step-by-step.
4. Verify device time is accurate (TOTP is time-sensitive).
5. Offer alternative MFA methods (SMS/email) as backup.
6. If hardware token issue, replace the security key.

title: Oracle deadlock detected in Ticket System
description: Frequent deadlocks occurring in Elasticsearch when Monitoring Dashboard processes concurrent order updates. 28 deadlocks in the last hour. Transaction rollbacks affecting user experience.
resolution:
1. Analyze deadlock logs to identify the conflicting transactions.
2. Review application locking order — ensure consistent acquisition order.
3. Reduce transaction scope and duration.
4. Add appropriate row-level locking hints.
5. Implement retry logic for deadlock errors in application code.
6. Consider optimistic locking where possible.

title: Malware detected on endpoint prod-db-12
description: Endpoint protection (CrowdStrike) flagged a ransomware on qa-web-27 used by pooja.sharma@company.com. File: svchost_helper.dll. Status: partially removed.
resolution:
1. Immediately isolate the affected machine from the network.
2. Run a full system scan with the endpoint protection tool.
3. Check if the malware has spread to other machines on the network.
4. Reset all credentials used on the affected machine.
5. Reimage the machine if the infection is severe.
6. Submit the malware sample to the threat intelligence team.

title: Mobile app crashing on iOS for HR Dashboard
description: The mobile app for Reporting Engine is crashing immediately on launch for Android 14 users. Crash reports show SEGFAULT. Approximately 1684 crash reports in the last 10 hours.
resolution:
1. Pull crash reports from Crashlytics/Sentry.
2. Identify the crashing stack trace and affected code path.
3. Release a hotfix build with the crash protection.
4. If API-related, add backward compatibility on the server side.
5. Push the fixed build to App Store/Play Store as an expedited review.

title: Suspicious login attempts detected for amit.lee@company.com
description: Security alert: 20 failed login attempts detected for account pooja.williams@company.com from IP 42.209.121.42 (unknown VPN). Account may be under brute-force attack. Current status: still active.
resolution:
1. Temporarily lock the affected user account.
2. Block the source IP at the WAF/firewall level.
3. Reset the user's password and require MFA reenrollment.
4. Review login audit logs for any successful unauthorized access.
5. Notify the user and their manager about the incident.
6. Add the IP range to the threat intelligence blocklist.

title: AWS service quota exceeded in eu-central-1
description: Hitting service quota limits for NAT Gateways in Azure eu-west-1. Cannot provision new resources for SRE.
resolution:
1. Review current quota usage in AWS console.
2. Submit a service quota increase request.
3. Clean up unused/orphaned resources.
4. Consider using a different region for non-critical workloads.
5. Implement resource tagging and lifecycle policies.

title: Unauthorized access to AWS console detected
description: CloudTrail/Activity Log shows unauthorized API calls from an unrecognized IP 191.48.139.243 using IAM user admin. Actions include: ListBuckets. Credentials may be compromised.
resolution:
1. Immediately rotate/deactivate the compromised IAM credentials.
2. Review CloudTrail logs for all actions taken by the compromised user.
3. Revert any unauthorized changes (new users, policies, resources).
4. Enable MFA on all IAM accounts if not already enabled.
5. Implement IP-based access restrictions for console access.
6. Conduct a full IAM access review.

title: Mobile app crashing on iOS for CRM Portal
description: The mobile app for API Gateway is crashing immediately on launch for iOS 17 users. Crash reports show NullPointerException. Approximately 893 crash reports in the last 5 hours.
resolution:
1. Pull crash reports from Crashlytics/Sentry.
2. Identify the crashing stack trace and affected code path.
3. Release a hotfix build with the crash protection.
4. If API-related, add backward compatibility on the server side.
5. Push the fixed build to App Store/Play Store as an expedited review.

title: Monitoring alerts flooding from sandbox cluster
description: Receiving 306 alerts per hour from Terraform for the production environment. Alert fatigue is causing the Frontend team to miss real issues. Most alerts are for high memory.
resolution:
1. Review and categorize current alert rules.
2. Increase alert thresholds for non-critical metrics.
3. Add alert grouping and deduplication rules.
4. Implement alert silencing for known maintenance windows.
5. Create tiered alerting: P1 pages, P2 Slack, P3 dashboard only.

title: Permission request for GCP resources
description: alex.garcia@company.com from Security requesting read-only access to Azure Lambda functions in DR environment. Business justification: compliance audit.
resolution:
1. Verify the request and manager approval.
2. Create/identify an appropriate IAM role or group.
3. Grant scoped access following least-privilege principle.
4. Set an access expiration date if this is temporary access.
5. Document the access grant in the access management log.
6. Notify the user that access has been provisioned.

resolution: 1. Check if the equipment is properly connected to the power source. 2. Contact the IT department for assistance.

title: Phone Voicemail Not Working
description: My phone voicemail is not working.
resolution: 1. Check if the voicemail is properly set up. 2. Contact the IT department for assistance.

title: Desktop Background Not Changing
description: My desktop background is not changing.
resolution: 1. Check if the background image is properly selected. 2. Ensure the desktop settings are not locked by the IT department.

title: Laptop Not Charging
description: My laptop is not charging.
resolution: 1. Check if the charger is properly connected. 2. Try using a different charger or outlet.

title: Webcam Not Working
description: My webcam is not working.
resolution: 1. Check if the webcam is properly connected. 2. Ensure the webcam settings are not blocked by the IT department.

title: Software Installation Request
description: I need a specific software installed on my computer.
resolution: 1. Contact the IT department to request the software installation. 2. Provide the software name and version.

title: Network Connection Lost
description: I lost my network connection.
resolution: 1. Check if the network cable is properly connected. 2. Restart the router and try connecting again.

title: Email Not Sending
description: I'm having trouble sending emails.
resolution: 1. Check if the email settings are properly configured. 2. Ensure the email account is not blocked by the IT department.

title: Computer Restarting Randomly
description: My computer is restarting randomly.
resolution: 1. Check if the computer is overheating. 2. Ensure the computer is properly ventilated and the fans are working.

title: Printer Not Recognized
description: My computer is not recognizing the printer.
resolution: 1. Check if the printer is properly connected. 2. Ensure the printer drivers are up-to-date.

title: Conference Room TV Not Working

title: Email Account Locked

title: Printer Paper Jam
